title: Semantic Model Relationship to Metrics Across Multiple Platforms
story: As a data analyst, I want to see which semantic models a metric is implemented in across platforms like Salesforce, Tableau, and Snowflake, so that I can understand metric coverage, discover gaps, and manage cross-platform metric consistency.
description:
Semantic models have become a critical component of the modern data stack, and a single metric can exist across multiple semantic model platforms. This feature establishes the relationship between a metric in the DataTools Pro glossary and its implementations across various semantic model providers.

## Supported Platforms

- **Salesforce** — already integrated as a metric source
- **Tableau Metrics** — already integrated as a metric source
- **Tableau Semantic Model** — planned integration
- **Snowflake Semantic Model** — planned integration
- **Fabric Semantic Model** — future consideration

## Capabilities

1. **One-to-many relationship** — a single glossary metric can be linked to implementations in multiple semantic models across different platforms.
2. **Source tracking** — the existing `source` field on metric records indicates which platform the metric was imported from.
3. **Cross-platform discovery** — users can see where a metric has been implemented, where it has not, and what it is called in each system (alias consolidation).
4. **Merge and deduplication** — when the same metric exists in multiple systems under different names, users can consolidate, merge, and manage these as a single logical metric.
5. **Future: translation and deployment** — the long-term vision includes translating metric definitions between semantic model formats and helping users deploy metrics from one platform to another.

## Visualization (Future)

A visualization showing a metric and all connected semantic models where it lives, with per-model naming and status.
acceptance_criteria: A metric can be linked to implementations in multiple semantic model platforms
The source field indicates which platform each metric implementation came from
Users can view all semantic model platforms where a metric is implemented
Users can see the alias/name of the metric in each platform
Existing Salesforce and Tableau metric source integrations continue to work
Users can identify metrics that exist in some platforms but not others (gap analysis)
Merge and deduplication workflows allow consolidating the same metric across platforms
ux_changes: New UI surface for viewing and managing cross-platform semantic model relationships for a metric. Users can see which platforms a metric is implemented in, the alias in each platform, and perform merge/deduplication operations.
middleware_changes: Logic to manage one-to-many relationships between glossary metrics and semantic model implementations. Merge and deduplication workflows for consolidating metrics across platforms. Source tracking leverages the existing source field on metric records.
data_model_changes: Relationship model extended to support one metric linking to many semantic model implementations across platforms. Each implementation record stores the source platform, platform-specific metric identifier, alias/name in that platform, and status.
api_changes: Endpoints for retrieving semantic model relationships for a metric, linking/unlinking implementations, and performing merge/deduplication operations across platforms.
implementation_notes: The existing `source` field on metric records provides the foundation for platform identification. The implementation builds on this to establish a one-to-many relationship between a glossary metric and its implementations across semantic model providers. The Salesforce and Tableau integrations already exist; Tableau Semantic Model and Snowflake are being actively planned. Fabric is a future consideration. The developer noted that the existing source field can be leveraged for the solution but requested guidance on UI implementation.
comment_summary: Developer noted that the current model already has a source field for metrics from Salesforce, Tableau, etc., which can be leveraged. Requested clarification on the UI approach for displaying and managing cross-platform semantic model relationships.
